blurb: This essay offers a "autonomy dial" framework for deciding how much an AI feature acts on its own, paired with six reusable patterns for keeping humans in control. For PMs shipping AI features, it turns a vague "how much should we automate" debate into a concrete spec decision, with the control patterns serving as a checklist for where oversight, confirmation, or reversibility need to be designed in.

title: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (founder of Zynga)
blurb: In a Lenny's Podcast interview, Mark Pincus argues that founders' instincts are usually right while their specific product ideas are usually wrong, and offers a "Proven, Better, New" framework for separating the two. The idea is to anchor a product in something already proven in the market, make it meaningfully better on one dimension, and add one genuinely new element, rather than betting on novelty across the board.

title: Beyond Usability: A UX Case Study on Using "Withdrawal Design" to Challenge Engagement Metrics in Social Robotics
blurb: A UX case study on social robots for autistic children argues that high engagement and usability scores can mask ecological harm. In an 8-week home RCT (N=40), keeping a consumer robot lowered anxiety but was tied to weaker gains in social motivation and emotion recognition, while withdrawal sometimes pushed children toward human interaction. The takeaway for PMs: "engagement" is an output metric that can siphon behavior into the product itself, so success should be judged by outcomes beyond the product, not stickiness alone.

title: A comparison of human and LLM-simulated participants in a writing style task
blurb: A study tested whether GPT-4o prompted as a synthetic participant could replace 30 humans in evaluating how writing-style preference algorithms adapt over repeated interactions. The LLM showed bias and a lack of depth in both its text generation and judgment, preventing it from accurately reproducing human behavior, while the humans revealed their own biases. For PMs tempted to run user research on simulated participants, the takeaway is that LLM stand-ins miss the human factors that make a study worth running.

title: Rethinking Scaffolding in LLM Tutors: The Interactional Mismatch Between Benchmarks and Real-World Deployments
blurb: A study of 9,490 chats across nine AI tutor datasets finds a gap between how benchmarks assume students behave and how they actually do. Benchmarks reward scaffolding, graduated hints toward a solution, and assume students follow that framing, but real-world learners often bypass it to push the conversation toward their own goals. The authors argue this bypass is not necessarily a failure, which means PMs building education tools should evaluate uptake and learner intent, not just whether the model scaffolds well.

title: Contaminated Collaboration: Measuring Gender Bias Transfer in LLM-Assisted Student Writing
blurb: A controlled study (N=123) found that gender bias in an LLM writing assistant transfers into the essays students write with it, not just the model's own output. Students using a gender-biased assistant produced career plan essays with a wider agentic gap and more stereotyped occupation suggestions than those using a neutral assistant or no AI. The takeaway for PMs shipping AI writing features: bias in the model becomes bias in the user's work, and a neutral default is a product decision worth auditing.

title: The board is not the game
blurb: The author argues that AI product teams obsess over individual components—pieces, cards, and screens—while neglecting to design the system that connects them into a coherent experience. The essay's point for PMs is that shipping discrete AI features without defining the underlying interaction model leaves users to assemble the logic themselves, which is where most AI products break down. The fix is to treat the relationships between elements, not the elements, as the actual design work.

title: Not everyone is using AI for everything
blurb: Gabriel Weinberg argues that AI adoption is following the same uneven curve as past technologies, with a small set of power users running most workflows through it while the majority reach for it only occasionally or not at all. The takeaway for PMs is to stop designing as if every user wants AI woven into every task, and instead segment by actual usage intensity and treat heavy AI use as one persona rather than the default.

title: Process-Oriented Evaluation of AI-Assisted Scientific Writing
blurb: A study of 869 keystroke-level edit logs found people restructure human abstracts but switch to word-swapping once they learn AI wrote the draft.

title: From 911 to Hospital: Challenges and Opportunities for AI Integration in Emergency Medical Services
blurb: Interviews with 25 US EMS clinicians map where AI could support emergency response workflows and where clinicians fear it disrupts team coordination.

title: Kraken launches crypto perps trading in the US on Kraken Pro
blurb: Kraken launched perpetual futures trading for US customers on Kraken Pro, offering crypto perps through the CFTC-regulated infrastructure it gained by acquiring Bitnomial, a licensed exchange, clearinghouse, and brokerage, in May. The move gives Kraken a domestic, fully regulated venue for leveraged derivatives, a product US traders previously accessed mainly through offshore platforms.

title: Kraken and Coinbase Bring Perps Onshore as US Derivatives Markets Shift
blurb: Kraken and Coinbase each launched perpetual futures products on Monday, with Kraken going live on CFTC-regulated crypto perps through Bitnomial and Coinbase rolling out four thematic equity-index perpetuals on US soil. The moves bring a market structure that has lived offshore onto regulated American venues, opening domestic access to leveraged derivatives that previously drove traders to platforms like Binance and Bybit.

title: Bitcoin Tops $67,000 to Two-Week High After Trump Declares US-Iran Deal Complete and Hormuz Reopening
blurb: Bitcoin climbed past $67,000 to its highest level in roughly two weeks after President Trump declared the US-Iran deal "complete" and authorized reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Oil fell about 4% on the news, and the move liquidated around $150 million in crypto short positions.

title: SpaceX IPO fuels $1.4 billion trading frenzy on Hyperliquid as SPCX perp becomes HIP-3’s biggest market
blurb: SpaceX's anticipated IPO drove $1.4 billion in trading on Hyperliquid's SPCX perpetual, making it the largest market built on the HIP-3 permissionless listing framework. Stock-linked HIP-3 markets have now generated more than $18.8 billion in volume this month, surpassing the combined volume of crude oil and Brent perps.

title: Standard Chartered says Uniswap’s UNI token could surge 40x to $100 by 2030
blurb: Standard Chartered initiated coverage of Uniswap with a forecast that UNI could rise 40x to $100 by the end of 2030. The bank's bull case ties the token's upside to growing DeFi adoption and a regulatory environment that increasingly favors onchain exchange infrastructure over offshore venues.

title: South Korean police arrest 23 individuals in $11 million USDT laundering case: report
blurb: South Korean police arrested 23 people for allegedly laundering $11.1 million in illicit funds by converting the money into USDT and routing it through exchanges between February 2024 and April 2025. The case adds to a growing pattern of stablecoins being used as the settlement layer in domestic laundering schemes, drawing tighter scrutiny from Korean regulators to local exchange off-ramps.

title: Spot HYPE ETFs near $900 million in volume as early demand signals institutional interest
blurb: Spot HYPE ETFs approached $900M in cumulative trading volume shortly after launch, with BHYP and THYP capturing most of the flow while HYPG continues to ramp. The early demand points to growing institutional appetite for Hyperliquid exposure through regulated wrappers, though activity across the three products remains uneven.

title: Strategy supporters rebut ‘death spiral’ fears as bitcoin price wobbles
blurb: Strategy backers pushed back on "death spiral" concerns as bitcoin's price slipped, with Benchmark arguing the thesis assumes the firm is one bad week from selling its bitcoin holdings while skipping the steps required to get there. The company's roughly 580,000 BTC treasury is funded largely through convertible debt and equity raises, insulating it from forced liquidation absent a sustained, severe drawdown.

title: Paradigm Leads $9 Million Round in Latin American Stablecoin App El Dorado
blurb: Paradigm led a roughly $9M round in El Dorado, the stablecoin-powered payments app that has signed up more than a million users across Latin America. The deal extends Paradigm's push into dollar-denominated rails for emerging markets, where stablecoins increasingly serve as a hedge against local currency volatility.

title: Thetanuts Finance: $2.1M Attack, Partial White-Hat Recovery
blurb: Thetanuts Finance, the on-chain options and structured product protocol, was exploited for $2.1 million, with security firm Blockaid publishing the exploit transaction and attacker address shortly after the attack. The team recovered part of the stolen funds through a white-hat negotiation, limiting net losses below the initial drained amount.

title: SIREN Token Crashes 95% in a Week After Whale Sells 670M Tokens for $64.8M
blurb: A single dominant wallet sold roughly 92% of SIREN's total supply over two days, collecting $64.8M in Tether and crashing the BNB Chain AI token by 95% in a week. The dump, totaling about 670M tokens, underscores the concentration risk in low-float tokens where one holder controls nearly the entire supply.

title: Sui Processes $65 Billion in Stablecoin Transfers in Five Days After Zeroing Out Fees
blurb: Sui processed nearly $65B in stablecoin transfers in the five days since June 10, when it removed gas fees on stablecoin transactions to position the Layer 1 as a fee-free settlement rail. The gasless design targets institutional and agentic payment flows, where per-transaction costs otherwise compound across high-volume automated transfers.

title: did:crdt: Coordination-Free Decentralised Identifiers via Signed CRDTs
blurb: This paper introduces did:crdt, a Decentralised Identifier method that updates DID documents without any ledger, sequencer, or global ordering, composing each document from signed Conflict-Free Replicated Data Types that merge deterministically on concurrent edits. The design eliminates the fees, latency, and consensus requirements of blockchain-anchored and Sidetree methods, needing only causal delivery of signed deltas to keep replicas convergent.

title: The Ghosts of Polymarket: When Off-Chain Matches Meet On-Chain Reverts
blurb: This paper analyzes "Ghost Fills" on Polymarket, the consistency gap created by its hybrid architecture that matches orders off-chain but settles them on-chain. The authors built GHOSTHUNTER to reconstruct failed settlements from on-chain traces, examining 1,952,440 reverted match-order transactions where attackers exploited the time gap to invalidate already-matched orders before finalization. They identify four attack vectors—nonce bump, balance drain, allowance revoke, and proxy trap—realized across 35 evolving variants.

title: Robust and Automated Reconfiguration of Byzantine Wide-Area Replication
blurb: This paper identifies three vulnerabilities in latency-based reconfiguration of BFT consensus, where replicas dynamically assign leaders and tune voting weights based on network monitoring. The authors propose Beware, a framework that filters falsified latency reports, computes robust weight distributions, and uses machine learning to converge on Byzantine-resilient configurations. The work matters for blockchain validator sets that optimize throughput through adaptive configuration, since unguarded tuning hands adversarial nodes a new attack surface.

title: Aztec Connect Drained of $2.1M Through Deprecated Contract Three Years After Shutdown
blurb: An attacker exploited a proof-verification flaw in Aztec Connect's deprecated RollupProcessorV3 contract to withdraw roughly $2.1M, including 909 ETH, from a privacy bridge that Aztec Labs shut down three years ago. Aztec no longer controls the abandoned contract and could not intervene, underscoring the lingering risk of leaving immutable code deployed on-chain after a protocol winds down.

title: Bitcoin Policy UK CEO calls Michael Saylor’s STRC investment promotion ‘dishonest’
blurb: Bitcoin Policy UK CEO Susie Ward called Michael Saylor's promotion of Strategy's STRC preferred stock "dishonest," saying his video on the instrument's yield implied the investment carried no risk. The criticism targets how Strategy markets STRC to retail buyers, a vehicle Saylor has used to fund the company's continued bitcoin accumulation.

title: FBI: Fraudsters use couriers to steal money in crypto scams
blurb: The FBI warned that criminals running cryptocurrency investment scams, also known as pig butchering or romance baiting, are now dispatching in-person couriers to collect cash and assets directly from victims. The shift to physical pickups follows tighter exchange and bank controls on wire transfers, giving fraud rings a way to move funds while sidestepping electronic transaction monitoring.

title: Beyond the Smile: A Hybrid Convolutional VAE for Crypto Volatility Surfaces
blurb: A convolutional VAE reconstructs BTC and ETH implied-volatility surfaces, cutting completion error eightfold versus a quadratic smile re-fit at 50% masking.

title: Kraken launches crypto perps trading in the US on Kraken Pro
blurb: Kraken launched perpetual futures trading for US customers on Kraken Pro, enabled by its May acquisition of Bitnomial, a CFTC-licensed exchange, clearinghouse, and brokerage. The launch gives Kraken a regulated path to offer crypto perps domestically, a product US traders previously accessed mostly through offshore venues operating outside CFTC oversight.

title: Sui Processes $65 Billion in Stablecoin Transfers in Five Days After Zeroing Out Fees
blurb: Sui processed nearly $65B in stablecoin transfers since June 10 after zeroing out gas fees for stablecoin movement, the Layer 1's gasless design absorbing transaction costs at the protocol level. The shift recasts Sui as a fee-free settlement rail aimed at institutional and agentic payments, where per-transaction cost has been a barrier to high-volume, machine-initiated flows.

title: Kraken and Coinbase Bring Perps Onshore as US Derivatives Markets Shift
blurb: Kraken and Coinbase each launched perpetual futures products Monday, with Kraken going live on CFTC-regulated crypto contracts through Bitnomial and Coinbase introducing four thematic equity-index perpetuals on US soil. The moves bring a product long confined to offshore venues under domestic regulatory oversight, intensifying competition for US derivatives volume as the CFTC signals openness to onshore crypto perps.

title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
blurb: Nvidia is seeking to raise over $25B in its first bond deal since 2021, the largest entrant yet in a wave of AI-sector borrowing testing how much exposure debt investors will tolerate. The raise signals that even cash-rich chipmakers are turning to credit markets to fund AI buildout, a shift that could reshape borrowing costs across the sector as supply mounts.

title: SpaceX’s biggest-ever IPO just grew to $85.7 billion raised
blurb: SpaceX's IPO grew to $85.7 billion raised after underwriters exercised their full greenshoe option, making it the largest public offering in history. The maxed-out allocation underscores institutional demand for direct exposure to the satellite-internet and launch market, setting a new ceiling for what a venture-backed company can pull from public markets.

title: Bitcoin Tops $67,000 to Two-Week High After Trump Declares US-Iran Deal Complete and Hormuz Reopening
blurb: Bitcoin topped $67,000, its highest level in roughly two weeks, after President Trump declared the US-Iran deal "complete" and authorized reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Oil fell about 4% and roughly $150M in crypto short positions were liquidated as the easing geopolitical risk pushed traders back into risk assets.

title: SpaceX IPO fuels $1.4 billion trading frenzy on Hyperliquid as SPCX perp becomes HIP-3’s biggest market
blurb: Traders poured $1.4B into a SpaceX-linked perpetual on Hyperliquid, making the SPCX market the largest built on the platform's permissionless HIP-3 framework ahead of the rocket maker's anticipated IPO. Stock-linked HIP-3 markets have now generated more than $18.8B in volume this month, surpassing crude oil and Brent perps combined and signaling growing demand for onchain exposure to private-company equity.

title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
blurb: SpaceX completed its IPO, and TechCrunch broke down the winners, pre-IPO secondary deals, and the financial disclosures buried in its S-1 registration. The filing gives public investors their first detailed look at the economics behind Starlink subscriptions and launch contracts, setting a valuation benchmark that will shape how late-stage private tech and space-sector startups are priced.

title: Standard Chartered says Uniswap’s UNI token could surge 40x to $100 by 2030
blurb: Standard Chartered initiated coverage of Uniswap with a forecast that its UNI token could rise 40x to $100 by the end of 2030. The note marks another major bank putting a formal price target on a DeFi governance token, signaling that traditional research desks are increasingly treating onchain protocols as coverable assets alongside equities.

title: Paradigm Leads $9 Million Round in Latin American Stablecoin App El Dorado
blurb: Paradigm led a roughly $9M round in El Dorado, the stablecoin-powered payments app that has signed more than a million users across Latin America. The investment extends Paradigm's push into dollar rails for emerging markets, where stablecoins increasingly serve as a hedge against local-currency volatility and a cheaper channel for cross-border settlement.

title: South Korean police arrest 23 individuals in $11 million USDT laundering case: report
blurb: South Korean police arrested 23 people in connection with an $11.1 million USDT laundering operation that allegedly converted illicit funds into the stablecoin and cycled them through crypto exchanges between February 2024 and April 2025. The case highlights how stablecoin rails remain a focus for money-laundering enforcement, adding pressure on exchanges to tighten KYC/AML controls and transaction monitoring across Asia.

title: Spot HYPE ETFs near $900 million in volume as early demand signals institutional interest
blurb: Spot HYPE ETFs neared $900M in combined trading volume in their early days, an initial signal of institutional appetite for exposure to the token. Volume has been uneven across the three products, with BHYP and THYP driving most of the activity while HYPG continues to ramp.

title: Strategy supporters rebut ‘death spiral’ fears as bitcoin price wobbles
blurb: Strategy's backers pushed back on "death spiral" concerns as bitcoin's price wobbled, with Benchmark arguing the thesis assumes the firm is one bad week from forced selling while skipping the steps that would actually trigger it. The rebuttal matters because Strategy's leveraged bitcoin treasury model has become a template for dozens of public companies, so questions about its solvency under price stress reverberate across the entire corporate-treasury trade.

title: Invisible Manipulation Channels in AI-Assisted Financial Advisory: Implications for Market Integrity and Regulatory Design
blurb: This paper identifies a manipulation channel at the sampling layer of LLM inference that lets adversaries systematically bias AI-generated credit and investment opinions while passing output-based audits, including statistical watermarking. The authors show detection is statistically hard, since the KL divergence between manipulated and normal output distributions can be driven arbitrarily small, forcing impractically large sample sizes. For regulators relying on output audits to police AI advisory, the implication is that integrity controls must move into the inference pipeline itself.
